title: Structured Observability, Health, Profiling, Capacity, and Incidents · Language Lens: Profile Capacity Incident
summary: Logs contain tokens and raw paths, metrics use user IDs, traces mark every 404 as a server error, readiness ignores database saturation, and a p50 benchmark drives release. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo profile capacity incident increment in low-cardinality service evidence and incident dashboard. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use benchmarks, pprof in a protected plane, traces, saturation, tail latency, allocation, contention, load shape, and changed-fault evidence for capacity and incidents.
Separate liveness, readiness, startup, dependency, drain, and deep diagnostics; bound their cost; and prevent sensitive disclosure.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Profile Capacity Incident · Theory Profile Capacity Incident
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 9713 in a signed fulfillment webhook gateway under replay attack. Build deterministic Go evidence to use bounded route telemetry and distinguish server errors from readiness capacity; then change the tenant and show deny-by-default before repository access. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
